tests.text: Given a valid YAML with one group and one permission rule, produce a Config struct with correct group members and one parsed rule
Given groups with 'include' references (A includes B), resolve membership: checking A returns members of both A and B
Given a group containing 'evm:0xABC...', store it as a valid EVM identity in the group's member list
Given a permission rule referencing 'founders', link it to the 'founders' group defined in the groups section
Given a permission rule referencing 'nonexistent' (no matching group), return error: 'unknown group: nonexistent'
Given group A includes B and group B includes A, detect the cycle and return error: 'circular dependency: A → B → A'
Given groups nested 6 levels deep (A→B→C→D→E→F→G), return error: 'max include depth exceeded (5)'
Given '>main' in a permission target, classify it as a branch target with pattern 'main'
Given 'contracts/**' in a permission target (no > prefix), classify it as a path target with glob 'contracts/**'
Given 'contracts/** >main' in a permission target, classify it as a combined target: path 'contracts/**' AND branch 'main'
Given a .repobox.yml with groups defined but empty permissions section, produce a valid Config with no rules (everything unrestricted)
Given 'not edit' in a permission rule, mark the rule as deny=true with verb='edit'
Given invalid YAML (e.g. bad indentation), return a parse error with the line number where it failed
Identity evm:0xAAA is in founders. Rule: 'founders push >main'. Check push to main → returns ALLOW
Identity evm:0xCCC is NOT in founders. Rule: 'founders push >main'. Check push to main → returns DENY (implicit: someone else is explicitly allowed)
Identity evm:0xAgent is in agents. Rule: 'agents push >feature/*'. Check push to 'feature/my-thing' → returns ALLOW (glob matches)
Identity evm:0xAgent is in agents. No rule mentions >main. Check push to main → returns DENY
Identity evm:0xAgent is in agents. Rule: 'agents create >feature/*'. Check create branch 'feature/new' → returns ALLOW
Identity evm:0xAgent is in agents. Rule: 'agents create >feature/*'. Check create branch 'release/v1' → returns DENY (pattern doesn't match)
Identity evm:0xDev is in devs. Rule: 'devs merge >develop'. Check merge into develop → returns ALLOW
Identity evm:0xAgent is in agents. No merge rule exists for any branch. Check merge into anything → returns DENY
Rule: 'evm:0xBob push >main' (direct identity, no group). Check evm:0xBob push main → ALLOW. Check evm:0xAlice push main → DENY
evm:0xBob is in devs. Rule 1: 'devs push >main'. Rule 2: 'evm:0xBob not push >main'. Check Bob push main → DENY (explicit deny overrides group allow)
Commit touches 'src/app.rs'. No file rules in config. Check → returns ALLOW (no restrictions on this path)
Commit touches '.repobox.yml'. Rule: 'founders edit .repobox.yml'. Identity is in founders → ALLOW. Identity is in agents → DENY
Commit touches 'package.json'. Rule: 'devs edit package.json'. Identity is in devs → ALLOW. Identity is in agents → DENY
Commit touches 'contracts/Vault.sol'. Rule: 'devs edit contracts/**'. Identity is in devs → ALLOW (glob matches)
Commit touches 'contracts/Vault.sol'. Rule: 'devs edit contracts/**'. Identity is in agents (not devs) → DENY
Commit touches both 'src/app.rs' (unrestricted) and '.repobox.yml' (restricted to founders). Identity is agents → DENY (all files must pass)
evm:0xBob is in devs. Rule: 'devs edit contracts/**'. Rule: 'evm:0xBob not edit contracts/**'. Check Bob edits contracts/X.sol → DENY (explicit deny)
Rule 1 (line 10): 'evm:0xAlice edit .repobox.yml'. Rule 2 (line 20): 'devs not edit .repobox.yml'. Alice is in devs. Check Alice edits .repobox.yml → ALLOW (rule 1 is higher, wins)
Rule 1 (line 10): 'founders not merge >main'. Rule 2 (line 20): 'devs merge >main'. Identity is in both groups. Check merge to main → DENY (rule 1 is higher, wins)
Same permission rule appears at line 10 and line 30. Evaluation uses line 10 (first occurrence wins)
Run 'git repobox init' inside a git repo → creates .repobox.yml template file + writes real git path to .repobox/config
Run 'git repobox init' in a directory without .git → returns error: 'not a git repository'
After 'git repobox init', .repobox/config contains the absolute path to the real git binary (e.g. /usr/bin/git)
After shim is installed, running 'git --version' → delegates to real git, returns real git version string
Shim is invoked in a repo WITHOUT .repobox.yml → all commands pass through to real git unchanged (transparent)
Run 'git repobox identity set 0xPRIVATEKEY' → derives EVM address, stores in ~/.repobox/identity, prints 'Identity set: evm:0xABC...'
Run 'git repobox identity set 0xPRIVATEKEY --alias alice' → sets identity AND registers alias alice in ~/.repobox/aliases
Run 'git repobox whoami' with alias set → prints 'alice (evm:0xABC...)'
Run 'git repobox whoami' with NO alias set → prints 'evm:0xABC...' (raw address)
Identity file is stored at ~/.repobox/identity (global, not per-repo)
Run 'git commit' with no identity set → returns error: 'no identity configured. Run: git repobox identity set <private-key>'
Run 'git commit' in a repo with no .repobox.yml → delegates to real git, commit succeeds normally
Run 'git commit' with .repobox.yml present, all staged files are allowed for this identity → delegates to real git, commit succeeds

Run 'git commit' with .repobox.yml present, staged file '.repobox.yml' is restricted to founders, identity is agents → BLOCKED before real git runs. Error: 'permission denied: evm:0xAgent cannot edit .repobox.yml'
Run 'git commit' with multiple staged files, one is restricted → BLOCKED. Error message names the specific restricted file
Run 'git commit --no-verify' with restricted file → still BLOCKED (shim is not a hook, --no-verify is irrelevant)
Run 'git commit -m "my message"' with allowed files → '-m' flag is passed through to real git, commit message is set correctly
Run 'git commit --amend' → shim re-checks the full amended diff against permissions before delegating
On branch 'main', run 'git merge feature/x'. Identity has merge permission for >main → delegates to real git, merge succeeds
On branch 'main', run 'git merge feature/x'. Identity lacks merge permission for >main → BLOCKED. Error: 'permission denied: evm:0xAgent cannot merge into main'

Merge would bring in changes to '.repobox.yml' from feature branch. Identity can merge the branch but can't edit .repobox.yml → BLOCKED. Error: 'merge contains changes to .repobox.yml which you cannot edit'
Run 'git merge --no-ff feature/x' with permission → '--no-ff' flag passed through to real git
Fast-forward merge (no merge commit needed) → same permission checks still apply
Run 'git push origin feature/x'. Identity has push permission for >feature/* → delegates to real git
Run 'git push origin main'. Identity lacks push permission for >main → BLOCKED. Error: 'permission denied: evm:0xAgent cannot push to main'
Run 'git push --force origin main'. No force-push permission for >main → BLOCKED. Error mentions force-push specifically
Run 'git push' with no explicit branch. Shim detects current branch from HEAD, checks permissions for that branch
Run 'git checkout -b feature/new'. Identity has create permission for >feature/* → delegates to real git, branch created
Run 'git checkout -b release/v1'. Identity lacks create permission for >release/* → BLOCKED
Run 'git checkout feature/existing' (switching to existing branch, not creating) → passes through, no permission check needed
Run 'git branch new-branch' → same create permission check as 'git checkout -b'
Run 'git branch -d feature/old' → checks delete permission for >feature/* before delegating
Run 'git status' → passes through to real git unchanged, no permission checks
Run 'git log', 'git diff', 'git add', 'git stash', 'git fetch', 'git clone' → all pass through unchanged
Run 'git pull' that triggers a merge → the merge portion gets permission-checked (same as git merge)
Run 'git whatever-unknown-command' → passes through to real git (unknown commands are not intercepted)
Repo has no .repobox.yml file → ALL git commands pass through unchanged (shim is completely transparent)
.repobox.yml exists but has a YAML syntax error → permission-checked commands (commit/merge/push) are BLOCKED with parse error. Read-only commands (status/log/diff) still pass through
Shim is invoked outside any git repository → passes through to real git (which will show its own error)
Binary is invoked as 'box' (not 'git') → shows box-specific help: init, whoami, identity, serve
Run 'git repobox alias add claude evm:0xBBB...456' → writes to ~/.repobox/aliases
Run 'git repobox alias list' → prints all aliases with addresses
Run 'git repobox alias remove claude' → removes from ~/.repobox/aliases
Aliases file (~/.repobox/aliases) is per-machine, not per-repo
Permission denied errors show alias when available: '❌ claude cannot edit .repobox.yml' instead of raw address
git log --show-signature shows alias: 'EVM-signed by claude (evm:0xBBB...456)'
Sub-agent alias uses plus notation: 'git repobox alias add claude+roudy-piglet evm:0xCCC...789'
'git repobox keys generate --alias claude+roudy-piglet' generates key AND registers the alias in one step
If no alias exists for an address, CLI falls back to showing raw evm:0x... address
Alias names cannot start with special characters (no @ or % prefix needed)
Run `git repobox init` inside a git repo → creates .repobox.yml template with empty groups and default:allow permissions
The generated .repobox.yml template includes commented examples showing flat and nested rule syntax
Run `git repobox init` when .repobox.yml already exists → error: ".repobox.yml already exists. Use --force to overwrite"
Run `git repobox init --force` when .repobox.yml exists → overwrites with fresh template
Run `git repobox init` outside a git repo → error: "not a git repository"
Run `git repobox keys generate` → creates new key pair in ~/.repobox/keys/<address>.key, prints address
Run `git repobox keys generate --alias claude` → creates key AND registers alias in ~/.repobox/aliases
Generated key file has 0600 permissions (owner-only read/write)
Run `git repobox keys import <hex>` → stores key, derives and prints address
Run `git repobox keys import <invalid-hex>` → error with clear message
Run `git repobox keys list` → prints all key addresses in ~/.repobox/keys/
Run `git repobox identity set <private-key>` → derives address, stores in ~/.repobox/identity, sets user.signingkey in git config
Run `git repobox identity set <private-key> --alias alice` → sets identity AND registers alias
Run `git repobox whoami` with alias → prints "alice (evm:0xAAA...)"
Run `git repobox whoami` without alias → prints "evm:0xAAA..."
Run `git repobox whoami` with no identity set → error: "no identity configured"
`git repobox check alice push >main` → resolves alias to address, checks permission, prints result with rule that matched
`git repobox check evm:0xBBB... edit .repobox.yml` → prints ❌ denied with reason (implicit deny or explicit rule)
`git repobox check` with no .repobox.yml → error: "no .repobox.yml found"
`git repobox lint` with valid config → prints "✅ .repobox.yml is valid" + summary (N groups, N rules)
`git repobox lint` with unknown group ref → prints error with line info
`git repobox lint` warns about unreachable rules (broader allow above narrower deny for same subject)
Binary installed as git shim (PATH override or git alias). Running `git status` → delegates to real git transparently
Running `git commit` with .repobox.yml present → shim checks file permissions before delegating
Running `git push origin main` where identity lacks permission → prints error and exits non-zero (real git never invoked)
Running `git repobox <subcommand>` → routes to repobox CLI, not to real git
Shim reads identity from user.signingkey in git config (standard git config resolution)
Shim discovers real git binary path (not itself) to delegate to. Stores path in .repobox/config on init
Error messages include alias when available: "❌ claude cannot push to main" instead of raw address
When gpg.program = repobox, git commit calls repobox to sign. Repobox signs the commit data with the secp256k1 private key
Signed commit contains a valid EVM signature that can be verified with ecrecover
`git verify-commit HEAD` with repobox as gpg.program → verifies the EVM signature, prints signer address
`git log --show-signature` shows "EVM-signed by alice (evm:0xAAA...)" for signed commits
Signing uses the key from user.signingkey config (not hardcoded)
If the signing key file is missing from ~/.repobox/keys/ → clear error: "key not found for evm:0xAAA..."
Setup: `git repobox setup` creates ~/.repobox/bin/git symlink. When PATH includes ~/.repobox/bin, all git commands route through repobox
Agent with no push permission runs `git push origin main` → exit code 1, stderr shows "❌ denied — agent-1 push >main"
Agent tries to commit changes to .repobox.yml (no edit permission) → blocked BEFORE commit is created
Agent with `push >feature/**` permission runs `git push origin feature/test` → succeeds, real git executes
Agent runs `git checkout -b feature/new` (has create permission) → succeeds
Agent runs `git checkout -b hotfix/urgent` (no create permission for hotfix/) → blocked
Blocked commands never reach real git — no partial state, no broken repo
If .repobox.yml is missing or invalid, git commands still work (permissive fallback for non-repobox repos)
Error messages include the agent alias if known: "claude+roudy-piglet" not raw "evm:0xBBB..."
`git commit --no-verify` still checks permissions — shim is not a hook, cannot be bypassed
`git push --no-verify` still checks permissions — shim intercepts before git even runs
Shim detects real git location (stored in ~/.repobox/real-git), never calls itself recursively
